title: Adani Power Earns High ESG Rating from NSE Sustainability Assessments - Machine Maker
content:
Adani Power Limited, India’s largest private-sector power generation company, has received strong recognition for its environmental, social, and governance performance from NSE Sustainability Ratings and Analytics Limited. The assessment agency, a subsidiary of NSE Indices Limited under the National Stock Exchange of India group, evaluated the company using publicly available disclosures and awarded Adani Power a score of 65, placing it in the “Aspiring” category.
In this evaluation, Adani Power ranked above other leading thermal, mixed-fuel, and integrated energy companies assessed under the same framework. The result highlights the company’s consistent focus on embedding sustainability into its operations and decision-making processes, alongside its commitment to responsible growth.
The rating reflects Adani Power’s sustained efforts across a broad spectrum of ESG initiatives. On the environmental front, the company has adopted advanced emission-reduction technologies, including Ultra Super Critical boiler systems, to improve efficiency and lower its carbon footprint. Continuous emissions monitoring and corrective mechanisms have been deployed across operations to align performance with global best practices. Water stewardship is another priority area, with the company implementing zero liquid discharge systems to significantly reduce freshwater consumption and minimize impact on local water resources.
From a social perspective, Adani Power has expanded its community outreach programs with a focus on education, healthcare, and skill development. These initiatives include scholarship schemes for students from economically weaker sections, regular health camps, and livelihood enhancement projects aimed at supporting sustainable community development. Internally, the company places strong emphasis on employee well-being through structured safety training, workforce development programs, and initiatives to promote diversity and inclusion across its locations.
In terms of governance, Adani Power exceeds several regulatory benchmarks. The composition of key board committees, including the Nomination and Remuneration Committee, Audit Committee, and Risk Management Committee, features independent director representation above statutory requirements. The company has also extended its ESG expectations to suppliers and contractors by implementing robust standards across its value chain, reinforcing responsible business conduct beyond its own operations.
The NSE Sustainability assessment follows other recent independent evaluations of Adani Power’s ESG performance. Sustainalytics has assigned the company an ESG Risk Rating of “Medium Risk” with a score of 29.2, outperforming the global electric utility industry average of 36.9, where lower scores indicate lower risk. Additionally, CSRHub awarded Adani Power an ESG rating of 77 percent, significantly higher than the global industry average of 51 percent. These external assessments reinforce Adani Power’s long-term commitment to strengthening ESG performance while creating sustainable value for all stakeholders.
Adani Power Limited, part of the Adani portfolio, is India’s largest private thermal power producer. The company operates an installed thermal capacity of 18,110 MW across twelve power plants located in Gujarat, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, Jharkhand, and Tamil Nadu, along with a 40 MW solar power plant in Gujarat. Through technology adoption, innovation, and operational excellence, Adani Power continues to support India’s transition toward reliable, affordable, and sustainable electricity.
